always.exam: Fracture/tendon-injury screen (deformity, bony tenderness, active DIP/PIP extension) if any trauma
always.why: A traumatic finger/thumb presentation must exclude fracture, tendon rupture (mallet/boutonniere) or a thumb UCL Stener lesion before provocative testing.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Screens for fracture / tendon injury requiring imaging or referral

always.exam: Inspection + palpation of the digits (DIP/PIP nodes, thumb MCP, palmar fascia, A1 pulley) with active flexion/extension
always.why: Precise joint- and structure-level findings are the primary discriminator between nodal OA, trigger finger, Dupuytren's and collateral ligament injury.
always.confirmsOrExcludes: Localises the symptomatic digit and structure

always.exam: Grip and pinch strength + functional hand use
always.why: Quantifies impairment and helps differentiate mechanical/structural sources from neurological ones (which route to the wrist/cervical screen).

add.exam: Thumb MCP valgus stress test (flexion and extension) + imaging
add.why: Ulnar collateral ligament injury is confirmed by valgus laxity; imaging excludes an avulsion fracture / Stener lesion that needs surgical referral.

add.exam: A1-pulley palpation for a nodule + observe active triggering
add.why: Palpable catching at the A1 pulley with triggering confirms stenosing flexor tenosynovitis.

supporting.exam: Hueston tabletop test (if a palmar cord is present)
supporting.why: A positive tabletop test indicates a functionally significant Dupuytren's contracture and informs referral timing.

supporting.exam: Cervical / wrist neurological screen if any numbness or tingling
supporting.why: Digit paraesthesia is usually a nerve-entrapment (CTS/cubital tunnel) or cervical source rather than a local hand joint problem.
supporting.confirmsOrExcludes: Differentiates neurological referral from a local hand source
